taxonomy: Uragoga lucens belongs to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family is part of the order Magnoliales and class Magnoliopsida in the kingdom Plantae. Uragoga lucens is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The classification is further broken down to the genus Uragoga, which is a small group of plants. The Annonaceae family comprises over 2,000 species of plants, many of which are economically important.
